# kslides

> kslides is a Kotlin DSL for creating [reveal.js](https://revealjs.com) presentations. Slides are authored in Markdown, HTML, or the Kotlin HTML DSL. Output can be static HTML files (for Netlify/GitHub Pages) or served dynamically via an embedded Ktor HTTP server.

## Quick Start

New presentations should be created from the [kslides-template](https://github.com/kslides/kslides-template) repository. The template includes build configuration, deployment setup, and a starter presentation.

## Core Concepts

### DSL Structure

A presentation is defined using nested Kotlin DSL blocks:

```kotlin
kslides {
  output {}                 // Configure filesystem or HTTP output
  kslidesConfig {}          // Global kslides settings
  presentationConfig {}     // Default reveal.js config for all presentations
  css {}                    // Global CSS (Kotlin CSS DSL or raw strings)

  presentation {
    path = "myslides.html"
    presentationConfig {}   // Override config for this presentation
    css {}                  // Presentation-specific CSS

    markdownSlide { content { "# Hello" } }
    htmlSlide { content { "<h1>Hello</h1>" } }
    dslSlide { content { h1 { +"Hello" } } }
    verticalSlides {
      dslSlide { content { h2 { +"Vertical 1" } } }
      dslSlide { content { h2 { +"Vertical 2" } } }
    }
  }
}
```

### Three Slide Types

- **markdownSlide** — Content is a Markdown string
- **htmlSlide** — Content is a raw HTML string
- **dslSlide** — Content uses kotlinx.html DSL (supports `playground{}`, `diagram{}`, `codeSnippet{}`, `letsPlot{}`)

`letsPlot{}` lives in the optional `kslides-letsplot` module; the others are in `kslides-core`.

### Configuration Cascade

Configuration merges hierarchically: global `presentationConfig{}` → per-presentation `presentationConfig{}` → per-slide `slideConfig{}`. Each level overrides its parent.

Sub-configs within `presentationConfig{}`: `menuConfig{}`, `copyCodeConfig{}`, `playgroundConfig{}`, `letsPlotIframeConfig{}`, `slideConfig{}`, `diagramConfig{}`.

### Output Modes

Configured in the `output{}` block:
- **Filesystem** (`enableFileSystem = true`): Writes static HTML to the `docs/` directory. Playground, Lets-Plot, and kroki content generates separate files in `docs/playground/`, `docs/letsPlot/`, `docs/kroki/`.
- **HTTP** (`enableHttp = true`): Starts a Ktor CIO server (default port 8080). Static resources served from `src/main/resources/public/`.

### DslSlide-Specific Features

- `playground {}` — Embeds an interactive [Kotlin Playground](https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin-playground) iframe
- `diagram {}` — Generates diagrams via [Kroki](https://kroki.io/) (PlantUML, Mermaid, Graphviz, etc.). The Kroki endpoint is configurable via `diagramConfig.krokiUrl`; a local Kroki server can be started from the `kroki/` directory with `docker-compose up -d` to avoid public-server rate limits.
- `codeSnippet {}` — Syntax-highlighted code block with reveal.js highlight plugin
- `letsPlot {}` — Embeds [Lets-Plot](https://lets-plot.org/kotlin/) visualization iframes (requires the `kslides-letsplot` module). The JS runtime version is configurable via `kslidesConfig.letsPlotJsVersion`.

### Utility Functions

- `include(src)` — Include code from a file path or URL (preferred over inline code)
- `githubRawUrl()` — Generate raw GitHub content URLs for `include()` calls
- `fragment()` — Wrap content in reveal.js fragments for animation
- `permuteBy()` — Generate animation step permutations
- `unorderedList()`, `orderedList()` — List helpers for DslSlides
- `headRow()`, `bodyRow()` — Table helpers for DslSlides

### CSS

CSS can be specified three ways:
1. Kotlin CSS DSL in `css {}` blocks
2. Raw CSS strings via `css += "..."`
3. External file at `src/main/resources/slides.css` (embedded into output)

CSS blocks can appear at global, presentation, or inline level. Unlike HTML, CSS blocks can be placed near the slides that reference them.

## Configuration Options

### PresentationConfig

All [reveal.js configuration options](https://revealjs.com/config/) are exposed as type-safe properties: `transition`, `transitionSpeed`, `controls`, `progress`, `center`, `loop`, `hash`, `autoSlide`, `slideNumber`, `mouseWheel`, `width`, `height`, `margin`, `theme`, `highlight`, etc.

kslides-specific options include: `title`, `theme` (enum: BLACK, WHITE, LEAGUE, BEIGE, SKY, NIGHT, SERIF, SIMPLE, SOLARIZED, BLOOD, MOON, DRACULA), `highlight` (MONOKAI, ZENBURN), `enableSpeakerNotes`, `enableMenu`, `enableCodeCopy`, `topLeftHref`/`topRightHref` (corner links), `gaPropertyId`.

### SlideConfig

Per-slide options: `backgroundColor`, `backgroundImage`, `backgroundSize`, `backgroundPosition`, `backgroundRepeat`, `backgroundOpacity`, `backgroundVideo`, `backgroundVideoLoop`, `backgroundVideoMuted`, `backgroundIframe`.

### Slide Properties

Common slide properties: `id`, `classes`, `style`, `hidden`, `uncounted`, `autoAnimate`, `autoAnimateRestart`. MarkdownSlides also support `filename` for external markdown files.

## Project Structure

- `kslides-core/` — Core DSL library
- `kslides-examples/` — Example presentations with main entry point
- `kslides-letsplot/` — Lets-Plot chart integration module

## Dependencies

Published to [Maven Central](https://central.sonatype.com/namespace/com.kslides) under the `com.kslides` group. Add to your project:

```kotlin
repositories {
    mavenCentral()
}
dependencies {
    implementation("com.kslides:kslides-core:1.0.0")
    // Optional: implementation("com.kslides:kslides-letsplot:1.0.0")
}
```

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/kslides/kslides/blob/master/README.md): Full DSL reference and usage guide
- [Template repo](https://github.com/kslides/kslides-template): Starting point for new presentations
- [Live demo (Netlify)](https://kslides.netlify.app): Example presentation output
- [Live demo (GitHub Pages)](https://kslides.github.io/kslides/): Example presentation output
- [Example source](https://github.com/kslides/kslides/blob/master/kslides-examples/src/main/kotlin/Slides.kt): Full example presentation code
- [reveal.js docs](https://revealjs.com): Underlying presentation framework
- [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/kslides/kslides/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md): Version history
